planning_gate.spec_delta: M19 tests knee/hip-biased actuator-space targets instead of scalar drop/blend increases.

perspectives.product: targets the remaining exp29 visible squat pose gaps directly
perspectives.architecture: reuses exp71 event recapture but biases IK target in knee and hip pitch actuator coordinates
perspectives.qa: native raw JSON per candidate plus visible 8cm, knee/hip, support, slip, return gates
perspectives.skeptic: extra joint flexion may close pose metrics but push the system into support/ZMP collapse

dod: run knee/hip-biased candidates around exp73 best recoverable timing
report whether pose bias closes visible gate or only moves the limiting gap

sources.note: Humanoid squat planning combines reference optimization and WBC tracking.
Squat-like WBC jointly considers CoM/ZMP, foot pose, and joint-limit objectives.
Unitree G1 developer guidance warns leg procedures should bring knees upright as much as possible, so deep knee bias remains a sim-only experiment until gated.
Unitree public G1-Comp specs list large knee and hip joint ranges, supporting kinematic plausibility of knee/hip-bias probes.
